wpcs_compliance.analysis: Uses tabs for indentation per WP standards. Function name uses camelCase instead of snake_case_with_underscores. Uses $this-> which is typical for class methods. Some variable naming conventions deviate from strict WP PHP coding standards.

security.analysis: Parses LESS/CSS content which is generally safe. Uses preg_match for matching. No file I/O or eval of user input. The LESS compilation could theoretically be heavy on memory for large inputs, but no direct injection vectors.

The push/pop block stack and recursive calls could cause memory issues with deeply nested CSS. No obvious O(n^2) loops but the parser nature means worst-case exponential without memoization.
